mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment spike, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series grow concurrently, or interaction term coefficient turns negative in quarterly regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions reflect capacity constraints and slowing real output, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 50% of instances following inflation spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market fragility and hidden job losses, which suppresses consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when unemployment rises alongside flat payrolls, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in quarterly models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases signal supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation, which erode near-term corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during recovery phases yield positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the regime-specific signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.
